title: GradeBook Class — OOP
description: Convert your program to use object-oriented design. Create a GradeBook class with a private vector of Student structs and public methods: addStudent(), displayAll(), getClassAverage(), saveToFile(), and loadFromFile(). The class should encapsulate all grade tracking logic.
test_code:
assert 'class' in cpp, 'Must define a class'
assert 'GradeBook' in cpp, 'Class should be named GradeBook'
assert 'private' in cpp, 'Must have a private section in the class'
assert 'public' in cpp, 'Must have a public section in the class'
import re
has_constructor = bool(re.search(r'GradeBook\s*\(', cpp))
assert has_constructor, 'Must define a GradeBook constructor'
assert 'addStudent' in cpp or 'add_student' in cpp, 'Must have an addStudent method'
assert 'displayAll' in cpp or 'display_all' in cpp, 'Must have a displayAll method'
assert 'getClassAverage' in cpp or 'get_class_average' in cpp or 'classAverage' in cpp, 'Must have a getClassAverage method'
acceptance_criteria: Defines a GradeBook class with private and public sections
Private section contains a vector or array of Student structs
File I/O methods are part of the class
main() creates a GradeBook object and uses its methods
hints: Class structure: class GradeBook { private: vector<Student> roster; public: GradeBook() {} ... };
Add a student: void addStudent(Student s) { roster.push_back(s); }
Class average: loop through roster, sum averages, divide by roster.size()
Don't forget the semicolon after the closing brace of the class definition
